name: Don't Pun
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 2: Meaningful Names
problem: The same word is reused for two semantically different operations (a pun), causing readers to assume identical behavior where the operations actually differ, leading to subtle bugs.
steps: Verify that every use of a shared method name (e.g., add) is semantically equivalent across all classes.
When an operation differs in meaning (e.g., inserting into a collection vs. summing values), use a different verb such as insert or append.
Prefer clarity over false consistency: do not reuse a term just because it seems consistent.
Review all classes that share a method name and confirm the semantics are truly the same.
Document any intentional reuse to prevent future misunderstanding.
